Results are written to scripts/cross-engine-drift-results.json (checked in, with a provenance field) -- the 'documented' half of the validation criterion. Wired into CI as a new 'Install Playwright browsers' + 'Cross-engine drift check (soft warn)' step pair after the P2.43 benchmark step in .github/workflows/ci.yml, running `pnpm exec playwright install --with-deps chromium firefox` then `pnpm check:cross-engine-drift`. Verified end-to-end locally against a real Chromium build (manually pointed at the sandbox's pre-installed revision, since Playwright's default resolution expects a newer pinned revision than the sandbox ships) -- measured drift is exactly 0 (Node and Chromium both run V8), recorded in the results JSON. Firefox could not be exercised in this sandbox (its network egress policy blocks the playwright.dev/CDN download hosts); the graceful-skip path was exercised for real here since Firefox has no sandbox build, and the actual Firefox measurement will happen on the real CI runner via the new playwright-install CI step.

tasks.title: Stiff-scenario telemetry: step-size trace channel h(t) recorded
tasks.validation: h(t) plotted artifact shows collapse near high-u phase
tasks.notes: Added packages/solverkit/src/step-size-recorder.ts: StepSizeRecorder, a Sink recording one (t, h) row per accepted step in a growing columnar store (same doubling-array pattern as TrajectoryRecorder), exposed via solverkit's index. Quantitative validation lives in packages/validation/src/stiff-step-size-collapse.test.ts: runs the dust-grain preset (P1.36, physically stiff Stokes-drag relaxation) through adaptive DOPRI5 with both StepSizeRecorder and TrajectoryRecorder attached, then asserts the h(t) collapse claim as concrete derived bounds rather than 'small numbers' -- max(h)/min(h) > 10x (measured ~21x), the h-minimum occurs within the first 5% of accepted steps (measured at step 1 of 550), and speed at that step exceeds half the launch speed (measured ~66% of u0=15 m/s), i.e. the collapse genuinely coincides with the high-speed phase, not just with t~0. The literal 'plotted artifact' is scripts/plot-stiff-step-size-trace.mjs, a standalone script (mirrors P2.43/P2.45's dist-consuming script convention) that runs the same fixture and hand-rolls a dependency-free log-log SVG line plot to scripts/stiff-step-size-trace.svg (checked in), marking the measured h-minimum point. Verified visually by rendering the SVG in the sandbox's Chromium via Playwright and inspecting a screenshot -- h dips to its minimum just after launch, rises sharply once the Stokes relaxation finishes, and plateaus for the rest of the flight, exactly the claimed shape.

tasks.title: Solver advisor rules v1 (table §4.10 as pure function of scenario groups)
tasks.validation: dust-grain ⇒ stiff warning; golf ⇒ DOPRI5 recommendation
tasks.notes: Added packages/engine/src/solver-advisor.ts: recommendSolver(spec), a pure function encoding 3 of §4.10's 5 regimes that are genuinely derivable from a ScenarioSpec alone -- "stiff", "conservation-focus" (gravity-only), and "default-adaptive" (the general sports-projectile case). The other 2 rows ("teaching order/convergence" and "ensembles/Monte Carlo") describe how the user is running the solve, not a scenario property, so no ScenarioSpec-only function can decide them; documented as explicitly out of scope for this v1 (left to P3.24's UI wiring). Stiffness is detected via a characteristic drag relaxation time tau (Stokes tau=m/(6*pi*eta*R) for linear drag; tau=v0/(2*g*Pi) for quadratic drag, derived algebraically from the existing dimensionlessPi rather than recomputing rho*Cd*A) compared against the flight's own timescale v0/g; threshold=50 was picked by measuring every P1.36 preset directly (non-stiff presets all land at ratio <5, dust-grain lands at ~2500 -- comfortably inside the two-orders-of-magnitude gap either way, not an assumed number). solver-advisor.test.ts: dust-grain triggers regime="stiff" with a warning matching /stiff/i (the task's literal validation criterion); golf-ball (drag+Magnus, not gravity-only) gets regime="default-adaptive"/recommendedStepperId="dopri5" with no warning (the criterion's other half); the drag-free reference gets "conservation-focus"/"velocity-verlet"; and every other preset (shot-put, table-tennis, golf, baseball) is asserted not spuriously flagged stiff.

tasks.title: Vertical-drop-from-rest kink case (C¹ point §3.8) convergence study
tasks.validation: measured RK4 order degradation documented (expect <4 locally)
tasks.notes: Added vertical-drop-kink-convergence.test.ts. Contrary to the blueprint's expectation, extensive measurement (global order over a full flight, local single-step order at the kink, and a spin/Magnus-coupled variant, all explored numerically before settling on the final test) shows RK4 does NOT degrade below its design order for this scenario: measured global slope ~4.0-4.05, local single-step slope ~5.0, both matching the away-from-the-kink benchmark in classical-rk4-stepper.test.ts. Mechanism: quadratic drag (and Magnus) are proportional to |v_rel|, vanishing at u=0, so gravity -- the only non-vanishing force exactly at the kink -- uniquely fixes the trajectory's departure direction (purely vertical). The anisotropic (direction-dependent) second derivative of the |v|*v map only bites a trajectory sampling both sides of the singularity (e.g. a direction-reversing crossing, not a one-sided departure from rest); along any single fixed ray from the origin, |v|*v reduces to r^2*d̂, which is analytic. Documented this as the measured, checked finding per the task's validation criterion ("documented", not assumed) rather than asserting a false degradation.

tasks.title: Property test: time-reversibility of Verlet on gravity-only (integrate forward then backward)
tasks.validation: returns to y₀ within 1e-9 over 100 steps
tasks.notes: Added verlet-time-reversibility.test.ts, both variants: 100 forward steps (h=0.01) on a gravity-only planar projectile model, then 100 backward steps (-h) from the same stepper, lands within 1e-9 of y0 (measured well under that, at plain floating-point roundoff level) -- distinct from and stronger than the existing bounded-energy-error test in verlet-stepper.test.ts, since reversibility specifically checks the recurrence undoes itself exactly under sign-flipped h, not just that energy stays bounded along the way. Includes a sanity assert that the forward leg actually displaced the state first, so the round-trip check isn't vacuously satisfied by a near-stationary trajectory.

tasks.title: Property test: translation invariance (shift x₀ ⇒ shifted trajectory)
tasks.validation: bit-equal after shift for wind-free scenarios
tasks.notes: Added translation-invariance.test.ts (fast-check, 100 random wind-free flights): shifting launch x0 by dx leaves y(t)/vx(t)/vy(t)/event-timing genuinely bit-identical (asserted with toBe, not close) -- correct, since nothing in the rhs or the flat-terrain ground-impact event reads absolute x when atmosphere/gravity/wind are position-independent, and y0/vx0/vy0 are unchanged between the two runs so those channels' whole recurrence is fed identical inputs. The x-channel itself is checked to a tight epsilon-scaled tolerance rather than literal bit-equality: measured directly during development (fast-check counterexamples, e.g. dx=-1e-6), x_new=x_old+increment accumulates via IEEE-754 addition that isn't associative across two accumulator paths differing by a constant offset -- (a+dx)+c can round to a different last bit than (a+c)+dx even though both equal the same real number -- so a handful-of-ULPs drift is an expected floating-point fact, not a physics defect, and asserting literal bit-equality there would be testing something false. Denormal-magnitude (~1e-300) generator values were also excluded (filtered to 0 or |v|>=1e-6): those lose enough relative precision to fail even the tolerant check for reasons unrelated to the property under test. A y0-shift variant was deliberately not added: flat terrain's ground-impact event is anchored at a fixed height, so shifting y0 changes event timing for a reason outside x-translation invariance. Follow-up (found while validating P2.51, unrelated task): the x-channel tolerance's flat `64 * Number.EPSILON * scale` bound turned out to be tight enough to intermittently fail (observed a handful of times in ~20 unseeded fast-check runs -- the flat constant didn't scale with step count, so worst-case accumulated rounding at late steps of the up-to-2000-step flight could exceed it by under 1%). Changed the bound to scale with step index (`* (i + 1)`), verified stable over 5 repeated runs plus every full-suite run since; still a tiny absolute bound (well under 1e-11 even at the last step) that only rules out real bugs, not legitimate FP accumulation.

tasks.title: SolverKit API docs + literate derivation pages (§4 content) co-located
tasks.validation: docs build; each stepper links its derivation
tasks.notes: Added packages/solverkit/typedoc.json (mirrors engine's: excludePrivate/excludeInternal, notExported/invalidLink/notDocumented validation, treatWarningsAsErrors) with projectDocuments: ["src/*.derivation.md"]. Wrote 9 literate derivation pages co-located with each stepper source file (explicit-euler, semi-implicit-euler, heun-rk2, midpoint-rk2, classical-rk4, bogacki-shampine-32, dormand-prince-54, verlet, backward-euler), each transcribing its blueprint §4 derivation (order-condition algebra, Butcher rooted-tree sketch for RK4, embedded-pair eq. 4.8-4.10 for BS32/DOPRI5, A-stability/Newton iteration for backward Euler, symplectic backward-error argument for semi-implicit-Euler/Verlet). Each stepper's top TSDoc comment links its page via a relative markdown link (typedoc rewrites these through projectDocuments; invalidLink+treatWarningsAsErrors makes a broken link fail the docs build). Fixed pre-existing undocumented-symbol/broken-link warnings elsewhere in the package (SOLVERKIT_PACKAGE, StepperId, ControllerKind, Sink call signatures, the three create*Buffers factories, createCancellationSource's inline return type, three internal-symbol {@link}s) so treatWarningsAsErrors passes cleanly. Added "docs" script + typedoc devDependency to solverkit's package.json, wired a "SolverKit API docs" CI step (mirroring the existing engine one). Validation: pnpm --filter @ballista/solverkit run docs builds clean (0 errors, 0 warnings); packages/solverkit/src/docs-derivation-links.test.ts asserts each of the 9 stepper files links an existing co-located *.derivation.md and that typedoc.json wires projectDocuments -- verified the generated HTML actually contains the resolved cross-links to each stepper's page.

tasks.title: Golden-trajectory store v1: 6 presets × {RK4 fixed, DOPRI5} recorded with hashes
tasks.validation: regression test compares within stated tolerances; update requires explicit flag
tasks.notes: Added packages/validation/src/golden-trajectory-store.ts: runGoldenTrajectory(presetId, stepperKind) builds a fresh Model/EvalContext/Stepper/SolverConfig from a PRESET_SCENARIOS entry and integrates it (mirrors solverkit's determinism.test.ts's runScenarioToTrajectory/forceById pattern, reused rather than re-abstracted since no spec-id resolver exists yet, P3.03+); hashTrajectory does SHA-256 over the Trajectory's backing buffers, same recipe as that file. Six presets selected (GOLDEN_PRESET_IDS): smooth-sphere, shot-put, table-tennis-ball, golf-ball, dust-grain, baseball-headwind -- one exemplar per §3.9 regime; tailwind deliberately excluded from v1 since it shares the same baseball projectile/physics as headwind and only flips the wind sign, not a materially different code path for a numerical-regression store (documented in the module). RK4 uses a fixed h per preset (0.005 s generally, 0.0002 s for the stiff dust grain per eq. 4.12's h<2*tau stability bound, tau~6e-4s); DOPRI5 uses rtol=1e-10/atol=1e-12/PI controller. Both integrate a fixed [0,2]s horizon (v1 scope: a numerical snapshot, not an event-terminated flight -- no ground-impact detection wired into the store). packages/validation/src/golden-trajectories.json is the checked-in fixture (schemaVersion, provenance note pointing back at the update command, 12 entries = 6 presets x 2 steppers): each entry has presetId/stepper/nSteps/hash/finalState; the smooth-sphere/classical-rk4 entry additionally stores its full t/channels arrays, satisfying §8.4's "hashes + full arrays for the small set" for at least one entry. golden-trajectories.test.ts is the regression test: normally (no env var) it loads the fixture and asserts, per entry, (1) recomputed nSteps matches, (2) the primary bit-exact SHA-256 hash matches (same-platform per §8.4), and (3) a hash-independent final-state check within §8.4's documented 1e-13 relative tolerance (a fallback that stays meaningful even where bit-exactness isn't expected), plus a dedicated full-array-equality test for the one entry that stores complete arrays. The explicit-flag update mechanism: when UPDATE_GOLDENS=1 is set, the same test file instead recomputes all 12 entries and overwrites the JSON fixture (verified: manually corrupting one entry's hash makes the normal test run fail with a clear diff, and re-running with UPDATE_GOLDENS=1 restores/regenerates it correctly). Added a root package.json script, "update-goldens": "UPDATE_GOLDENS=1 vitest run packages/validation/src/golden-trajectories.test.ts" (run from repo root, not via pnpm --filter, since the root vitest.config.ts's include glob is root-relative and doesn't resolve when vitest's cwd is a package subdirectory -- confirmed this the hard way earlier in the same session). No CI wiring needed beyond the existing pnpm test step, since the golden test is picked up by the root vitest run like every other package test. Per blueprint §8.4 and this repo's policy.quality, any future intentional numerical change here must re-run update-goldens with a commit message stating why results moved -- this initial recording's provenance note says so directly in the fixture itself.

tasks.title: App shell layout: canvas center, control dock right, analysis drawer bottom
tasks.validation: responsive at 1280/1920/mobile; no scroll traps
tasks.notes: Added packages/app/src/app-shell.{tsx,css}: AppShell composes canvas/control-dock/analysis-drawer regions via CSS Grid. >=768px (covers 1280/1920): fixed-viewport grid, canvas center, dock 320px column on the right, drawer spanning the bottom, dock/drawer independently overflow-y:auto. <768px (mobile): drops out of the fixed-viewport grid back into normal document flow (single page-level scroll surface) to avoid the classic mobile nested-scroll trap. App() now renders AppShell with placeholder region content (real content lands in P3.02+/P3.05+). Validation is a real-browser Playwright test (app-shell.responsive.test.ts) since jsdom doesn't run layout: builds the app via vite's build() API, serves it over a loopback HTTP origin via vite's preview() (file:// URLs block type=module scripts), then at 1280x800/1920x1080/375x812 checks no horizontal overflow, all three regions render with nonzero area, desktop dock-right-of-canvas/drawer-along-bottom geometry, and on mobile that the drawer becomes reachable by scrolling the page itself. Added playwright as an explicit packages/app devDependency (was only resolving via hoisting from the root). Reordered .github/workflows/ci.yml to install Playwright browsers before the Test step (previously installed only after, for the cross-engine-drift check), since this new test now also needs it. Note: discovered pnpm verify (typecheck/lint/lint:deps/test) is green on main, but three CI steps (bench:solverkit, check:cross-engine-drift, check-bundle-size) reference a scripts/ directory that does not exist in the current tree despite being referenced by package.json and having existed in the P2.05-era history -- likely lost in a prior history rewrite (origin/main required a forced ref update on fetch this run). Out of scope for this task; flagged for a future task to either restore or re-author those scripts.

tasks.title: Store setup: scenario/result/playback/ui stores per §5.4
tasks.validation: store unit tests; snapshot immutability asserted
tasks.notes: Added packages/runtime/src/{scenario,result,playback,ui}-store.ts, each a nanostores atom (per §6.4's tech choice) wrapping an immutable, Object.freeze'd snapshot with a small command API (setDraft/commit, publish/clear, setPlaybackTime/setSpeed/setLoop/play/pause/reset, setPanelCollapsed/setSelectedExhibits/setUnitsDisplay) -- no setter ever mutates the previous snapshot in place, only replaces the atom's value with a freshly frozen object, so a reference captured before a mutation stays valid and unchanged (asserted directly in tests, plus a TypeError-on-write check per store). Placed in `runtime` (L2), not `ui` (L4): viz (L3) needs read access to resultStore/playbackStore for rendering (§5.3 "Viz reads (Trajectory, playbackTime, cameraState)"), and viz's allowed imports (§2.1) don't reach as high as ui, so L2 is the only layer both viz and ui can import the stores from. compareStore (pinned trajectories) is intentionally not included here -- it's P3.something's dedicated 'Pin-trajectory compare' task, not named in this task's own title (only scenario/result/playback/ui are). resultStore.events is typed as readonly EventCandidate[] (solverkit) but always empty for now: integrate() doesn't yet surface non-terminal events on SolveReport (no EventCollector sink exists despite the P2.32 comment referencing one as future work) -- terminal events already appear as the trajectory's final row. uiStore's localStorage persistence is injected via a structural KeyValueStorage interface (get/setItem) rather than referencing globalThis.localStorage directly, so `runtime` needs no DOM lib (matches viz/ui/runtime's existing DOM-free tsconfigs; only `app` has one) -- the app package wires a real localStorage-backed adapter in when it builds the UI in a later task. 23 new tests (scenario/result/playback/ui-store.test.ts) cover initial state, that each command only ever changes its own field, atomicity where specified (commit, publish), and per-store frozen-snapshot + TypeError-on-mutation checks.

tasks.title: `SimulationSession` v1: commit spec → integrate (main thread) → publish result
tasks.validation: slider→result round trip < 16 ms (perf test on default scenario)
tasks.notes: Added packages/runtime/src/scenario-resolver.ts (spec-id -> live-instance resolvers: resolveForce/resolveModel build a Model+EvalContext+y0 the same way golden-trajectory-store.ts's local copy does; resolveStepper covers the v1-scoped fully-generic explicit steppers -- explicit-euler/midpoint-rk2/heun-rk2/classical-rk4/bogacki-shampine-32/dopri5 -- deliberately excluding Verlet (needs velocity-independent partitions to stay exact) and backward-Euler (needs a Jacobian) as not safe universal defaults for v1; resolveSolverConfig converts SolverConfigSpec's plain-array atol to Float64Array, using exactOptionalPropertyTypes-safe conditional spreads so unset fields are omitted rather than passed through as undefined) and simulation-session.ts (createSimulationSession: commitScenario resolves the spec, wraps the stepper in HermiteDenseOutputStepper when it lacks its own dense-output interpolant so the model's always-declared terminal ground-impact event can truncate the step, integrates on the main thread with TrajectoryRecorder+StatsCollector sinks, and publishes to resultStore only on success -- a failed solve leaves the previous result untouched and returns a typed CommitOutcome). T_MAX_SECONDS=60 is a v1 backstop tspan upper bound; the ground-impact event ends real scenarios well before it. Discovered and fixed a latent gap: every PRESET_SCENARIOS entry's solver.stepper was the string "rk45", which resolved to nothing anywhere in the codebase before this task (nothing had ever turned ScenarioSpec.solver.stepper into a live Stepper) -- resolveStepper accepts "rk45" as an alias for "dopri5" (the Dormand-Prince 4(5) pair this codebase implements under that id) rather than editing the already-"done" P1.36 preset fixtures. 38 new tests across scenario-resolver.test.ts and simulation-session.test.ts: every preset's forces/stepper resolve, atol array->Float64Array conversion, optional-field omission, default-scenario startup state, publish-on-success/no-publish-on-failure, ground-impact termination (final y within 1e-6 of 0, well under the 60s backstop), and the task's literal validation criterion -- a warm commitScenario(DEFAULT_SCENARIO) call on the default (drag-free reference) scenario measured well under 16ms (~1-2ms observed).

tasks.title: Draft/committed split with rAF-coalesced commits
tasks.validation: 100 rapid slider events ⇒ ≤ 1 solve per frame (instrumented)
tasks.notes: P3.02's scenario-store.ts already had the draft/committed split at rest; this task adds the missing rAF-coalescing half (§5.3: 'commits ... are scheduled per animation frame with latest-wins coalescing'). Added SimulationSession.updateDraft(spec) (simulation-session.ts): calls scenario.setDraft(spec) immediately (input rate, no solve), then schedules at most one frame via an injectable FrameScheduler; repeated calls before that frame fires only overwrite which spec is pending (latest-wins), so N rapid calls collapse into a single commitScenario/solve when the frame fires. Default scheduler probes globalThis.requestAnimationFrame dynamically (packages have no 'dom' lib, so the global isn't typed) and falls back to setTimeout(16ms) outside a browser. Restructured commitScenario/updateDraft as closures over a mutable `session` object reference so the scheduled callback calls session.commitScenario(...) -- letting tests instrument solve count via vi.spyOn(session, 'commitScenario') without adding a test-only counter to the public API. Added 2 tests to simulation-session.test.ts: 100 rapid updateDraft calls schedule exactly one frame and produce zero commits until it fires, then exactly one commit/solve with the latest (100th) draft's value carried through to committed (the literal validation criterion); and a scheduling-hygiene test (only one frame scheduled across repeated calls, firing a stale frame callback twice doesn't double-solve, a fresh updateDraft after the frame fires schedules a new one).

tasks.title: Canvas bootstrap: DPR-aware sizing, resize observer
tasks.validation: crisp on 2× displays; no distortion on resize
tasks.notes: Added bootstrapCanvas(canvas, options) to packages/viz/src/canvas-bootstrap.ts (new @ballista/viz module; added DOM/DOM.Iterable to its tsconfig lib -- L3 is the Canvas/WebGL rendering layer per §2.2, not under L0/L1's zero-DOM purity constraint). resize(cssWidth,cssHeight) sets canvas.width/height (backing store) to Math.round(css*dpr), sets canvas.style.width/height to the exact CSS-px box, and scales the 2D context transform by dpr so draw calls stay in CSS-pixel coordinates -- avoiding both the blurry-on-2x bug (unscaled backing store) and the stretched-CSS-box bug (backing-store size attributes overriding layout without an explicit style override) by construction. Watches for resize via ResizeObserver observing canvas.parentElement (falls back to the canvas itself only if detached) -- deliberately NOT the canvas itself, since resize() pins the canvas's own box via inline style, which would make an observer watching the canvas self-referential and silently stop firing after the first resize (caught by an end-to-end Playwright check during this task: the container visibly shrank on viewport resize but the canvas element's own box never moved). devicePixelRatio and the ResizeObserver constructor are both injectable (defaulting to a dynamic globalThis probe, since packages/viz's DOM lib doesn't guarantee these globals exist at runtime in every host) so canvas-bootstrap.test.ts can drive sizing and fire synthetic resize entries deterministically in vitest's node environment (7 tests, incl. a regression test pinning the parent-vs-self observation target). Wired a real <canvas data-testid="world-canvas"> into the app shell's canvas region via packages/app/src/canvas-viewport.tsx (useRef+useEffect: bootstrap on mount, dispose on unmount), replacing the P3.01-era placeholder text; canvas-viewport.test.ts is a real-Chromium Playwright test (jsdom doesn't implement devicePixelRatio/ResizeObserver/getBoundingClientRect layout faithfully) verifying the literal validation criterion at deviceScaleFactor:2 (backing store == CSS box × dpr, i.e. crisp) and across a page.setViewportSize resize (backing store keeps tracking the CSS box 1:1 at the same dpr in both dimensions, i.e. no distortion).

tasks.title: `Camera2D`: world↔screen transforms, pan (drag), zoom (wheel, cursor-anchored)
tasks.validation: round-trip transform property test; zoom keeps cursor point fixed
tasks.notes: Added packages/viz/src/camera2d.ts: Camera2DState = {centerX, centerY, scaleX, scaleY} (independent x/y scales -- anisotropic zoom, §6.1's 'essential when range >> height'), plus pure worldToScreen/screenToWorld (world y-up <-> screen y-down, the one place that flip lives so rendering and hover-picking share it), panByScreenDelta (shifts every rendered point's screen position by exactly the drag delta), and zoomAtScreenPoint (scales both axes by the same factor about a screen cursor point, solving analytically for the new center so the world point under the cursor stays under the cursor -- not iterative/approximate). Added fast-check as a viz devDependency (already used the same way in engine/solverkit) for camera2d.test.ts's two literal validation-criterion properties, 1000 runs each: screenToWorld/worldToScreen are exact inverses of each other across random camera+viewport+point; zoomAtScreenPoint keeps the cursor's world anchor point's screen position fixed across random camera+viewport+zoom-factor. Also covers: camera-center maps to viewport-center, the y-flip direction, independent x/y scaling, pan leaves scale unchanged, zoom preserves the scaleX:scaleY anisotropy ratio, and a directional sanity check that zooming in about the viewport center pushes off-center points further outward on screen.

tasks.title: Anisotropic auto-fit from trajectory bounds + padding; reset button
tasks.validation: full trajectory visible for all presets
tasks.notes: Added packages/viz/src/auto-fit-camera.ts, built on P3.06's Camera2D: computeBounds(xs,ys) (axis-aligned min/max of parallel x/y channel arrays, e.g. trajectory.channels[0]/[1]) and fitCameraToBounds(bounds,viewport,options) (independent x/y scale from each axis's own padded span -- true anisotropic fit, not a single uniform scale clamped to the tighter axis -- with a paddingFraction default of 10% margin per side and a minSpan default of 1 world unit guarding a degenerate zero-width axis, e.g. a dead-straight vertical drop's x span, from an infinite/absurd scale). Also encodes §6.1's 'user pan/zoom disables auto-fit until reset' as a small pure state machine -- CameraViewState={camera,autoFit}, fitToView (initial fit / what the reset button calls), applyManualCamera (installs a user-driven camera, sets autoFit=false), followBoundsIfAutoFitting (re-fits on new bounds/viewport only while still auto-fitting, a no-op once the user has taken over) -- since P3.02-P3.04's draft/committed store precedent is exactly this shape (plain data + pure transition functions, no hidden mutable object). auto-fit-camera.test.ts (14 tests) covers computeBounds edge cases (empty throws, single point), the anisotropic/padding/minSpan fitCameraToBounds behavior, the auto-fit/manual/reset state transitions, and the literal validation criterion: for every PRESET_SCENARIOS entry, actually integrates it via @ballista/runtime's createSimulationSession (real physics, not synthetic bounds), computes bounds from the resulting trajectory's x/y channels, fits a camera, and asserts every recorded (x,y) sample's worldToScreen position falls inside an 800x600 viewport for all 7 presets -- including the dust-grain (stiff, tiny-scale) and cannonball (large-scale) extremes the anisotropic scaling exists for.

tasks.title: TrajectoryLayer: Path2D polyline from columnar store
tasks.validation: drag-free preset renders parabola
tasks.notes: buildTrajectoryPath (packages/viz/src/trajectory-layer.ts) traces columnar x/y channels through the camera transform into a minimal PathBuilder interface (moveTo/lineTo), one moveTo + one lineTo per point; drawTrajectoryLayer is the thin browser-only Path2D wrapper. No decimation yet (P3.10). Validation test runs the drag-free preset, builds the screen-space path, and least-squares-fits a quadratic to it: R^2 > 1-1e-6 (an affine camera transform of a true parabola stays exactly a parabola) with a non-degenerate quadratic coefficient.

tasks.title: Screen-space RDP decimation on zoom change
tasks.validation: 50k-pt stiff run draws < 1 ms; max deviation < 0.5 px (test vs full path)
tasks.notes: rdpDecimateIndices (packages/viz/src/trajectory-decimation.ts): iterative (explicit typed-array stack, not recursive) screen-space RDP, tolerance 0.5px default. Perf-critical inner loop compares squared cross-products so sqrt/division run once per segment, not once per point; buildDecimatedTrajectoryPath inlines the world->screen transform to avoid a per-point object allocation. Validated: 50k-point stiff-solve-shaped trajectory (tiny steps during a fast transient, relaxing as the curve smooths) decimates+traces in <1ms JIT-steady-state (best-of-15 after 20 warmup calls); max deviation of dropped points from the simplified polyline measured <0.5px+1e-9 on a separate synthetic stiff-shaped signal. The 'only recompute on zoom change' caching this task's title references is the stateful wiring P3.11 (offscreen-canvas caching + dirty flags) owns -- this task delivers the pure, stateless decimation step it will call.

tasks.title: Offscreen-canvas caching of static layers + dirty flags
tasks.validation: steady-state frame cost < 4 ms (perf probe)
tasks.notes: packages/viz/src/static-layer-cache.ts: StaticLayerCache wraps a surface factory with a field-compared StaticLayerCacheKey (camera center/scale + viewport size + caller-supplied dataRevision) -- render() redraws into the offscreen surface only on a key or size change, otherwise returns the cached surface untouched; blitStaticLayerCache() does the one drawImage() a steady-state frame needs. Perf probe: 50k-pt trajectory decimation + axes-grid redraw as the stand-in expensive draw, best-of-15 render() calls with an unchanged key measured < 4 ms and redrawCount stays at 1 (the redraw never re-fires once camera/viewport/data stop changing).

tasks.title: ProjectileLayer: marker at playbackTime via dense output
tasks.validation: marker position matches interpolant to sub-pixel
tasks.notes: packages/viz/src/projectile-layer.ts: the recorded Trajectory carries no interpolation coefficients past the solve (only planarProjectileModel's [x,y,vx,vy] columnar rows), but since vx/vy literally are dx/dt, dy/dt, sampleProjectilePosition reuses P2.31's hermiteInterpolant directly on the bracketing recorded rows -- reproducing the live solver's own dense output for any fixed-step method, not approximating it. bracketStepIndex binary-searches the recorded times; theta is clamped to [0,1] so times outside the recorded span hold at the edge sample instead of extrapolating. Validated against ground truth: a second identical solve (fixed-h RK4 + HermiteDenseOutputStepper) with a probe Sink that calls stepper.interpolant directly on the accepted step bracketing a fixed playbackTime -- reconstructed position matches to 1e-10, well inside sub-pixel at any realistic camera scale.

tasks.title: Playback clock: play/pause/speed/loop; scrub bar with event ticks
tasks.validation: scrub to apex tick lands at v_y=0 state
tasks.notes: The blocking gap was that integrate() only ever root-localized *terminal* event crossings (ground-impact) -- non-terminal ones (apex) were detected but explicitly never localized/collected, per its own doc comments deferring that to 'a future EventCollector sink'. Implemented that sink: added Sink.event(root: EventRoot) to solverkit's types.ts, extended integrate()'s event-handling block to root-localize every candidate (not just terminal ones) and dispatch non-terminal roots to sinks -- but only those localized at-or-before the step's own earliest terminal crossing (a non-terminal crossing localized *after* a same-step terminal one never actually occurs). Added EventCollector (event-collector.ts, freeze-on-finish like TrajectoryRecorder). Wired into runtime: ResultStoreState.events is now EventRoot[] (was always [] via EventCandidate placeholder); SimulationSession.commitScenario attaches an EventCollector and publishes its events. Added the playback clock itself to SimulationSession (playback-store.ts's state existed since P3.02 but nothing advanced it): play()/pause()/scrubTo(t)/scrubToEvent(root), an injectable AnimationFrameScheduler-driven per-frame tick advancing playbackTime by dt*speed, looping (wraps modulo duration) or stopping+pausing at the trajectory's end, and play() restarting from 0 if already at the end and not looping. Added packages/viz/src/scrub-bar.ts: pure timeToFraction/fractionToTime, computeEventTicks (events -> sorted normalized tick positions), and snapToNearestEventTick for freehand drag/click to resolve onto a tick's exact time. Validation criterion is met by construction, not tolerance: scrubToEvent(apexRoot) sets playbackTime = apexRoot.t exactly, and apexRoot.y is the same root-localized state where v_y is ~0 by definition of the apex event (g(t,y)=v_y) -- verified in simulation-session.test.ts and integrate-events.test.ts (|v_y| < 1e-6 / 1e-9). No visual scrub-bar widget wired into app.tsx yet -- consistent with every other Phase-3 rendering task so far (P3.05-P3.12: camera, axes, trajectory layer, decimation, caching, projectile marker), which are all built and unit-tested as isolated modules with app-level wiring deferred; scrub-bar.ts is ready for that pass. pnpm typecheck/lint/lint:deps/test (534 tests)/build/bundle-size all green.

tasks.title: Force glyphs (F_g, F_d, F_M, resultant) log-scaled with legend
tasks.validation: glyph directions verified against rhs on 3 presets
tasks.notes: Model never exposes the individual ForceModels closed over its rhs (P1.17's registry is private), so per-force vectors can't be read off Model alone -- added `forces: readonly ForceModel[]` to runtime's ResolvedModel/resolveModel (scenario-resolver.ts), the live instances a caller already resolved from spec.model.forceIds, additive and non-breaking (also sets up P3.22's Forces panel magnitude badges to reuse it instead of re-deriving). Added packages/viz/src/force-glyphs.ts: computeForceGlyphs(model, forces, t, y, ctx, scratch) calls model.rhs once (refreshes ctx exactly as a real solve step would, and leaves ctx.forceAccum holding the exact resultant rhs itself divided by mass into out[VX]/out[VY]), then calls each ForceModel.accumulate against that same freshened ctx for its individual vector -- one source of truth between glyphs and physics, not a parallel reimplementation. logScaleGlyphLength/forceGlyphLegendTicks log-scale magnitude to an on-screen length (0 magnitude, e.g. no-spin Magnus, maps to 0 length -- nothing drawn -- vs. a floor length for a merely-tiny nonzero force). drawForceGlyphLayer + worldForceDirectionToScreen are the thin canvas-consumer half, following the AxesLayer split (pure compute + thin draw). Validation criterion met on 3 presets exercising all three force kinds (shot put: quadratic drag; golf drive: quadratic drag + Magnus; dust grain: linear drag): gravity is exactly (0, -mg); drag is antiparallel to v_rel (normalized cross ~0, dot ~-1); Magnus (golf drive) is perpendicular to v_rel (normalized dot ~0); the per-force sum equals the resultant, which divided by mass equals model.rhs's own acceleration output -- all to 1e-10, in force-glyphs.test.ts. No visual widget wired into app.tsx yet, consistent with every other Phase-3 rendering task so far (P3.05-P3.13), which stay isolated+unit-tested until a later app-wiring pass. pnpm typecheck/lint/lint:deps/test (547 tests)/build/bundle-size all green.

tasks.title: HUD readouts: t, |v|, E, Re, S, Π live
tasks.validation: values equal recorder channels at playhead
tasks.notes: packages/viz/src/hud-readout.ts: computeHudReadout (t, speed, energy, reynolds, spinRatio, pi at a state, mirrors force-glyphs.ts's model.rhs-refresh pattern) plus hudReadoutAtPlayhead, which snaps a playbackTime to its nearest recorded Trajectory row (no interpolation -- there's no recorded acceleration channel to Hermite-interpolate velocity from) and reads its exact channels, matching this task's literal validation wording. Added engine's spinParameter (characteristic-scales.ts) as the shared S = |omega|*R/|v_rel| formula, deduplicating it out of MagnusForce.accumulate/energyPower. While wiring spinRatio, found and fixed a real bug: resolveModel (runtime) and runGoldenTrajectory (validation) both called projectileSpecToParams(spec.projectile) without ever passing initialConditions.spin0 through, so ProjectileParams.spin was always undefined and Magnus lift silently never fired in any live-resolved integration (including the golf-ball golden trajectory) despite scenario-metadata.ts's independent spin-ratio computation and the P3.14 force-glyphs test both appearing to cover it. projectileSpecToParams now takes an optional spin argument; both call sites pass spec.initialConditions.spin0. This changes the golf-ball golden trajectory (Magnus force now genuinely applies -- final y goes from ~5.6 m to ~24 m, matching backspin lift): regenerated via `UPDATE_GOLDENS=1 pnpm run update-goldens` and re-formatted with prettier; only the golf-ball entries' hash/finalState/nSteps changed, every other preset's hash is untouched.

tasks.title: Hover picking: nearest trajectory point, full-state tooltip
tasks.validation: picked index correct under zoom (test with synthetic click)
tasks.notes: packages/viz/src/hover-picking.ts: pickNearestTrajectoryPoint measures distance in screen space (through the same worldToScreen every renderer uses), not world space -- required under Camera2D's anisotropic scaling, where world-space nearest and screen-space nearest can disagree. trajectoryPointTooltip builds the full-state tooltip from nothing but the recorded Trajectory plus the model's declared ChannelMeta (name/unit) -- no Model/EvalContext, no recomputed physics, per schema.ts's "Viz consumes only this plus recorded channel data". tasks.title: Schema-driven control generation (slider/number/select from zod meta)
tasks.validation: adding mock force with schema yields working controls, zero UI edits
tasks.notes: packages/ui/src/schema-controls.ts: generateControlDescriptors walks a zod ZodObject's own shape and derives each field's widget from what the schema itself already declares -- z.number().min/max -> slider (bounds needed for a track; one/no bound -> plain number), .step()/.multipleOf() -> step, z.enum -> select (options from the enum itself), z.boolean -> checkbox, .describe("Label|unit") -> label/unit (falls back to a humanized field name), .optional()/.default() unwrapped transparently. No per-field UI registry to keep in sync -- the widget kind is read entirely off the schema. Added zod as an explicit packages/ui dependency (previously only engine depended on it). Validated per this task's literal wording: a schema authored in the test file simulating a brand-new force's params (bounded slider, enum, boolean, unbounded number), run through the same unmodified generator already exercised against unrelated schemas in the other test cases, yields fully correct descriptors -- zero changes to generateControlDescriptors itself. Actual DOM/Preact rendering of these descriptors is later Phase 3 UI-panel tasks (P3.19+); this task's scope is the framework-agnostic descriptor data.

tasks.notes: packages/ui/src/projectile-panel-logic.ts (pure): findProjectilePreset looks up PROJECTILE_ASSETS by id; toCustomProjectileSpec seeds a custom spec (id='custom') from the *current* mass/radius/dragModel/liftModel rather than resetting to arbitrary defaults, which is the 'custom persists in draft' transition; hasEditableDragCoefficient narrows to only the constant-Cd case (a tabulated Re-curve has no single number to expose, skipped rather than guessed at, matching P3.18's schema-controls contract). projectile-panel.tsx: a <select> over PROJECTILE_ASSETS+Custom that swaps in a full preset ProjectileSpec on selection (so every selection -- including reselecting the current preset -- commits a fresh onChange call, i.e. re-solves), plus schema-driven mass/radius/dragCoefficient NumericControlRows (P3.18/P3.19) when the current projectile is custom. tasks.validation: badge equals |F| channel at playhead
tasks.notes: Added packages/ui/src/forces-panel.tsx + forces-panel-logic.ts: one toggle+badge row per KNOWN_FORCE_IDS (a new export added to packages/runtime/src/scenario-resolver.ts, the canonical id list resolveForce already maintained -- ResolvedModel's own doc comment had flagged this exact task as its future reader). tasks.title: Solver panel: grouped method dropdown, h/rtol/atol, controller select
tasks.validation: invalid combos (h with adaptive) prevented by schema
tasks.notes: Added packages/ui/src/solver-panel.tsx + solver-panel-logic.ts. Method <select> uses <optgroup> for Fixed-step/Adaptive (only the two groups scenario-resolver.ts's STEPPER_FACTORIES can actually resolve today -- geometric/implicit steppers exist in solverkit but aren't wired into the runtime resolver yet, per that file's own P4.10 note, so they're out of this panel's scope). integrate() (@ballista/solverkit) decides adaptive vs fixed purely from whether cfg.rtol is set, independent of cfg.h, and throws if an adaptive request targets a non-embedded-pair stepper -- so 'h with adaptive' is prevented structurally, not by refusing a bad value after the fact: fixedSolverPanelSchema (h only) and adaptiveSolverPanelSchema (rtol/atol/controller only) are disjoint, and toSolverConfigForStepper always rebuilds the committed SolverConfigSpec from only the new group's fields on a group-crossing switch (same-group switches, e.g. explicit-euler -> classical-rk4, just swap .stepper and keep existing values). Covered in solver-panel-logic.test.ts (toSolverConfigForStepper never leaves both h and rtol/atol/controller present) and solver-panel.test.tsx (a fixed stepper renders only the h row, an adaptive stepper renders only rtol/atol/controller, and switching between groups commits a spec missing the old group's fields entirely).

tasks.title: Pin-trajectory compare: pinned list, color assignment, legend
tasks.validation: Euler vs RK4 overlay reproduces §4 range-bias story visually
tasks.notes: Added compareStore (packages/runtime/src/compare-store.ts): pinned list (append/remove) with fixed-order categorical color assignment (8-hue palette, slot freed/reused on unpin, never cycled/regenerated -- 'color follows the entity, never its rank'), plus compareLegend() deriving swatch+label rows. Added drawPinnedTrajectoriesLayer (packages/viz/src/trajectory-layer.ts), a thin loop over the existing per-trajectory drawTrajectoryLayer/buildTrajectoryPath (P3.09) with each pinned entry's assigned color -- no change needed to the tested pure polyline-building core. Added CompareLegend (packages/ui/src/compare-legend.tsx), a stateless swatch+label list with an optional per-row unpin button, following the advisor-hint-panel.tsx pattern (call the component fn directly in tests, no DOM needed). Validation criterion test (packages/runtime/src/compare-euler-rk4.test.ts): runs the drag-free reference preset through explicit-euler and classical-rk4 at the same fixed h via a real SimulationSession, pins both, and checks the landing-range gap is (a) positive and visually significant (>1% of range) at a moderate h -- Euler's y_Euler(t) = y_exact(t) + 0.5*g*h*t closed-form bias always overshoots range on this drag-free case -- and (b) shrinks by ~4x when h is quartered, while RK4's own range barely moves, isolating the bias as Euler's O(h) global error (the §4.2 pitfall 1 'systematic range/apex bias' story) rather than run noise. A second test confirms pinning both runs produces a two-entry legend with distinct, in-order palette colors.
